items.prompt: Plot the point `(3, -2)` and explain what each coordinate controls.
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the slope between `(1, 4)` and `(5, 12)` and interpret it as a rate of change.
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Determine whether the table represents a function and justify your decision.
items.expected_evidence: explain the one-input-to-one-output condition in age-appropriate language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain the one-input-to-one-output condition in age-appropriate language with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how a table, graph, and equation can represent the same linear relationship.
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations.

items.prompt: Explain what slope means in both graphical and contextual terms.
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function.

items.prompt: A student reverses the coordinates and plots `(3, -2)` as `(-2, 3)`. Diagnose the mapping error. Focus on input output role blur.
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations.

items.prompt: A student says every straight-looking graph is proportional. Identify the misclassification. Focus on single output condition miss.
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function.

items.prompt: A ride-share charges a fixed fee plus a cost per mile. Write a rule, make a table, and interpret the slope and intercept.
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a graph and a verbal description, decide whether they could represent the same relationship and justify the match.
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, move between a rule, a table, and a graph for a linear relationship and explain the connection.
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on variable relationships and independent dependent quantities to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by function as input output rule itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether variable relationships and independent dependent quantities is secure enough to support function as input output rule
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in variable relationships and independent dependent quantities from a current-node issue in function as input output rule.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: variable relationships and independent dependent quantities

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on linear relationship tables and graphs to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by function as input output rule itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether linear relationship tables and graphs is secure enough to support function as input output rule
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in linear relationship tables and graphs from a current-node issue in function as input output rule.
